name: SAB Biotherapeutics, Inc.
business: SAB Biotherapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing multi-specific, high-potency human immunoglobulin G (hIgG) to treat and prevent immune and autoimmune disorders. Its lead product candidate, SAB-142, targets autoimmune type 1 diabetes with a disease-modifying approach. The company's proprietary platform uses transchromosomic cattle to produce human antibodies without the need for human donors.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing multi-specific, high-potency, human immunoglobulin G (hIgG) to treat and prevent immune and autoimmune disorders.

catalysts.event: Positive topline Phase 1 clinical results announced
catalysts.sentence: On January 28, 2025, we announced positive topline Phase 1 clinical results with the Company's potentially disease-modifying T1D therapy SAB-142.

catalysts.event: Additional Phase 1 clinical trial results announced
catalysts.sentence: We shared additional Phase 1 clinical trial results on December 17, 2025.

catalysts.event: Initiation of registrational Phase 2b SAFEGUARD trial
catalysts.sentence: We initiated our registrational Phase 2b clinical trial, called the SAFEGUARD study, in Q3 2025 and dosed the first patient in December 2025.

catalysts.event: First patient dosed in Phase 2b SAFEGUARD trial
catalysts.sentence: We initiated our registrational Phase 2b clinical trial, called the SAFEGUARD study, in Q3 2025 and dosed the first patient in December 2025.

catalysts.event: Type B meeting with FDA confirms intent to use SAFEGUARD data for regulatory approval
catalysts.sentence: On May 29, 2025, the Company held a constructive Type B meeting with the FDA... SAB confirmed its intent with the FDA to utilize the data from this study as primary evidence for future regulatory approval.

tamClaims.claim: An estimated 9.5 million people were living with T1D worldwide in 2024, and this number is predicted to increase to 14.7 million by 2040.
tamClaims.sentence: An estimated 9.5 million people were living with T1D worldwide in 2024, and this number is predicted to increase to 14.7 million by 2040.

tamClaims.claim: Nearly 1 in 300 children in the United States will be diagnosed with T1D during childhood, and one in every seven healthcare dollars can be attributable to the cost of managing diabetes over the lifetime.
tamClaims.sentence: Nearly 1 in 300 children in the United States will be diagnosed with T1D during childhood, and one in every seven healthcare dollars can be attributable to the cost of managing diabetes over the lifetime.
